title: BlackBerry Q2 2023 Earnings: What to Expect
text: For the quarter that ended August, analysts expected BlackBerry to lose 7 cents per share on revenue of $165.8 million, compared to the year-ago quarter when it lost 6 cents per share on revenue of $175 million. For the full year ending February 2023, the loss was expected to be 21 cents per share, reversing a profit of 18 cents a year ago, while full-year revenue of $730.14 million would decline 20.6% year over year. Shares of the Canadian software company had plunged more than 45% year to date and almost 50% over the past twelve months. Investors had become frustrated with the management's inability in recent quarters to generate much-needed cash from a patent portfolio sale. While the company had carved a solid niche within the security segment, the constant underperformance had raised doubts about the company's leadership to grow revenue in its Enterprise Software Services segment, and the Internet of Things segment had also underperformed.
